rationale: The page provides detailed technical information on Stratospheric Airship Platforms (SAP) and the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) program. It includes strategic advantages, program overviews, and a comparative table of fighter jet generations, which are highly relevant for science and technology assessments.
cleanedText:
It will enhance India’s earth observation and Intelligence, Surveillance & Reconnaissance (ISR) capability.
About the Stratospheric Airship Platform (SAP)
- A high-altitude, unmanned airship designed to operate in the stratosphere, typically between 17 to 22 kilometers above Earth.
- Functions as a High-Altitude Pseudo-Satellite (HAPS) that can remain airborne for extended durations.
- HAPS can be classified as aerodynamic (or heavier-than-air, e.g. fixed wing/airplanes) and aerostatic (or lighter-than-air, e.g. balloons and airships).
- Lift Mechanism: Uses helium for buoyancy.
- Power Source: Generally powered by solar energy with onboard batteries for night-time operation.
- Strategic Advantages: They can carry imaging sensors, radar, and telecom payloads, making them useful for border monitoring, disaster response, and intelligence missions.
- They bridge the gap between drones and satellites, providing space-like coverage with faster deployment, lower costs, and more flexibility.
6.7. AIRCRAFT/HELICOPTER
6.7.1. ADVANCED MEDIUM COMBAT AIRCRAFT (AMCA)
Why in the News?
Defence Minister approved Fifth-Generation Fighter Jet Execution Model through Industry Tie-Up.
More on the News
- India will jointly manufacture advanced jet engines for 5th generation fighter jet aircraft named Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) programme with French aerospace major Safran.
- Currently only the US (F-22 and F-35), Russia (Sukhoi Su-57), and China (Chengdu J-20) have developed operational fifth generation aircraft.
AMCA Programme Overview
- Lead Agency: Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) under DRDO.
- Core Capabilities: Supercruise, internal weapons bays, AI-driven avionics, radar-absorbing structures, etc.
- Variants: AMCA Mk1 with GE-F414 engines; Mk2 planned with indigenous engines.
Key Features of 5th Generation Fighters
- Powered by Twin-engine: Provides higher level of air safety and survivability in the event of failure of one engine especially at night.
- Stealth Capabilities: Harder to detect by enemy radar.
- Other: High maneuverability, fly at supersonic speeds, etc.
Other latest Generation of Jets
- Fourth generation jet fighters (1970- 1980s): Ability to both switch and swing roles between air-to-air and air-to-ground. Examples: MiG-29, F-16, Mirage-2000 etc.
- Four and half generation jet fighters: Added ‘stealth’, radar absorbent materials, thrust vector-controlled engines, to extend the range of fourth generation fighters. Examples: Eurofighter Typhoon, Rafale etc.
